text: Intro discovery — Conflux is evaluating Redwood for a hybrid deployment. Main concerns: network egress, VPC private endpoints, SSO/SAML, KMS integration, and preserving latency at high concurrency. They use LangChain and LlamaIndex heavily; want SDK adapter info, gateway/proxy patterns, and quota enforcement semantics. Agreed to run a short benchmark and exchange sample traces.

text: Discovery with HarbourPoint to understand inference needs for a customer-facing summarization product. Primary concerns: 99th-p latency, cost, data residency/PHI, and time-to-first-token (TTFT). Discussed hosted vs Dedicated vs Private (VPC/on-prem) tradeoffs, free credits quickstart, and next steps for a POC bench run.

title: Hemispheric routing & failover: KV cache scaling + on-prem SLA review
preview: Deep technical session: discussed a hemispheric routing approach to keep regional latency low while preserving cost; reviewed KV cache warmup and sticky routing tradeoffs; quantified batching thresholds vs p99 latency; worked through on-prem VPC handoff and audit/KMS requirements; agreed on a DR playbook draft and n...
text: Deep technical session: discussed a hemispheric routing approach to keep regional latency low while preserving cost; reviewed KV cache warmup and sticky routing tradeoffs; quantified batching thresholds vs p99 latency; worked through on-prem VPC handoff and audit/KMS requirements; agreed on a DR playbook draft and next engineering benchmarks.

text:
Header:
Date: 2026-03-18
Start: 11:00 UTC
Duration: ~75 minutes
Attendees: Ava Chen, Mateo Alvarez, Priya Nair, Liam O'Connor, Jordan Blake, Elena Park, Noah Wright, Sana Mir, Greg Foster

Summary:
Quick internal workshop to align on a concrete 'capacity staircase' (phased ramps), explicit rollback criteria for model and capacity regressions, and a short playbook for dry-run/drill execution. Focus was on launch readiness for the next private deployment wave and a plan to avoid cost surprises while keeping SLOs.

Topics:
- Capacity staircase definition (levels, triggers)
- Canary and dry-run plan
- Rollback criteria: quality regressions vs infra regressions
- Observability needs and runbook edits
- Security/audit notes for private deployments

Action items (captured live): see metadata section above.

title: On-prem air-gapped deployment discovery - MeduCo Health
preview: Discovery focused on air-gapped on-prem deployment constraints: sealed images, offline updates, hardware procurement windows, security controls (KMS/HSM, FIPS), and support model. Redwood explained Private/offline packaging, control plane options, and tradeoffs between hosted, dedicated, and air-gapped private insta...
text: Discovery focused on air-gapped on-prem deployment constraints: sealed images, offline updates, hardware procurement windows, security controls (KMS/HSM, FIPS), and support model. Redwood explained Private/offline packaging, control plane options, and tradeoffs between hosted, dedicated, and air-gapped private installs. Customer needs a deterministic update process for models and OS patches, signed artifacts, and minimal attack surface for regulated PHI workloads.
